codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all 10 expected behaviors. Backend adds 4 clean API endpoints (GET/PUT /api/claude-md, GET /api/context-preview, GET /api/projects) with proper error handling, graceful missing-file support, subprocess timeout, and parent-dir creation. Frontend implements a split-pane CLAUDE.md editor with debounced live markdown preview (regex-based renderer supporting headings, lists, bold, italic, code blocks, tables, horizontal rules) and a memory context preview panel with token budget slider, project filter dropdown, and regenerate button. 37 new tests added (all passing, 166 total) with comprehensive coverage of happy paths, error cases, and edge cases. Two non-blocking issues found.

issues.description: XSS gap in markdown renderer: renderInline() does not HTML-escape text before applying markdown transformations. Inline code (`), headings, list items, table cells, and paragraphs all render raw HTML. Only fenced code blocks use escapeHtml(). This violates the AGENTS.md convention 'HTML escaping is mandatory for all user-provided content rendered in the browser (XSS prevention)'. Risk is low since content is the user's own CLAUDE.md in a single-user local tool, but a malicious or accidentally pasted <script> tag would execute. Fix: apply escapeHtml() to text before inline markdown replacement, or escape non-markdown HTML tags.

Blocking subprocess.run() call inside an async handler (context_preview). This blocks the event loop for up to 30 seconds. For a single-user tool this is acceptable, but asyncio.create_subprocess_exec would be more correct for an async FastAPI endpoint.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md states 'HTML escaping is mandatory for all user-provided content rendered in the browser (XSS prevention)' but the markdown renderer in app.js only escapes HTML inside fenced code blocks, not in inline code, headings, paragraphs, list items, or table cells. This is an inherent tension with markdown rendering — you want to transform markdown syntax to HTML, but raw HTML in the source should still be escaped. AGENTS.md could clarify the convention for markdown preview contexts (e.g., 'escape HTML before applying markdown transformations, or use a whitelist approach').
sharedStateObservations.evidence: static/app.js renderInline() function (line ~1826) applies regex replacements for bold/italic/code without first escaping HTML. Compare to fenced code block handling (line ~1676) which does call escapeHtml().

summary: Feature passes review. All 10 expected behaviors are fully implemented with 37 well-structured tests. Backend endpoints are clean with proper error handling, graceful degradation, and subprocess timeouts. Frontend provides a functional split-pane markdown editor and context preview panel with interactive controls. Two non-blocking issues identified: (1) the custom markdown renderer doesn't HTML-escape non-code-block content before transformation, creating a theoretical XSS vector (low risk in this single-user context), and (2) blocking subprocess.run in an async handler. Neither issue warrants a failing grade given the single-user nature of the tool.
